skill.name: database-orm
skill.description: Guides database access for Neon Serverless Postgres projects that use Drizzle ORM.
skill.summary: Use Neon Postgres with Drizzle ORM for schema, queries, and migrations.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby or shell backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and TypeScript examples, not executable skill code. Real risks remain because the skill documents access to a remote Neon database through DATABASE_URL and includes migration commands that can alter database schema. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Database Migration Commands Can Modify Remote Schema
medium_findings.description: The skill documents drizzle-kit generate and push commands. These are legitimate Drizzle workflows, but push can change a connected database schema and should be reviewed before use against shared or production data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly shown as migration actions, and drizzle-kit push is intended to apply schema changes. The risk depends on the target database and execution context.

low_findings.title: Database URL Environment Variable Is Referenced
low_findings.description: The connection example reads process.env.DATABASE_URL. This is normal for database clients, but the value may contain credentials and must not be logged, pasted, or shared in prompts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable reference is direct and visible in the TypeScript example. It is legitimate configuration, but it carries normal secret-handling risk.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Most static external command alerts point to Markdown inline code, package names, table names, or TypeScript fenced code. These lines do not execute commands by themselves inside the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited material is documentation and TypeScript sample text, not Ruby backtick execution. No executable wrapper or script file is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Database Connection Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The sample initializes a Neon Serverless Postgres client from DATABASE_URL. This is expected for the skill, but it means prompts and generated code may interact with an external database.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code sample clearly creates a database client from an environment-provided connection string. The pattern is legitimate but security-relevant.

dangerous_patterns.title: Schema Migration Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill lists drizzle-kit commands for generating and pushing migrations. Running these commands can change project files or a connected database.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands are concrete CLI operations. Their impact is real if an assistant or user runs them with an active database connection.

content.user_title: Build Database Access with Drizzle ORM
content.value_statement: Teams need consistent guidance for connecting Neon Postgres to TypeScript applications. This skill explains the Drizzle ORM setup, schema locations, query patterns, and migration commands.
actual_capabilities: Explains how the Neon database client is initialized with Drizzle ORM.
Identifies where database connection and schema files belong in a project.
Shows basic select and insert query patterns using Drizzle.
Lists the app tables and authentication tables described by the project.
Documents Drizzle migration generation and push commands.
limitations: Does not include the full schema definitions for the listed tables.
Does not validate DATABASE_URL values or database permissions.
Does not provide rollback, backup, or production migration procedures.
Does not include automated tests for database queries.

use_cases.title: Set Up Database Access
use_cases.description: Use the skill to connect a TypeScript app to Neon Postgres through Drizzle ORM.

use_cases.title: Review Query Patterns
use_cases.description: Use the examples to align select and insert operations with the existing project structure.

use_cases.title: Prepare Schema Changes
use_cases.description: Use the migration notes to understand which Drizzle commands generate and apply schema updates.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Database Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how this project connects to Neon Postgres with Drizzle ORM. Include the role of db/index.ts and DATABASE_URL.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Query Example
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Drizzle ORM query for the existing users table. Follow the import and query style described by this skill.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a New Table
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a new Drizzle schema table for this project. Include fields, relationships, and migration steps, but do not run commands.

prompt_templates.title: Review Migration Safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned Drizzle migration for Neon Postgres. Identify data loss risks, required backups, and whether drizzle-kit push is appropriate.

output_examples.input: Explain how database access is initialized.
output_examples.output: The project creates a Neon SQL client from DATABASE_URL, then passes that client to Drizzle. Application code imports the shared database object from the db module.

output_examples.input: What should I check before pushing a migration?
output_examples.output: Confirm the target database, review generated changes, back up important data, check permissions, and avoid pushing directly to production without approval.

output_examples.input: Where are the main tables documented?
output_examples.output: The skill points to db/schema.ts for schema definitions. It lists authentication tables and application tables such as analyses and chatbot_history.

best_practices: Keep DATABASE_URL in environment configuration and never paste real credentials into prompts.
Review generated migrations before applying them to shared or production databases.
Use typed Drizzle imports from the project schema instead of handwritten SQL where possible.
anti_patterns: Do not run drizzle-kit push against production without backup and review.
Do not hardcode database connection strings in source files.
Do not change schema files without checking dependent queries and authentication tables.

faq.question: What database does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Neon Serverless Postgres used through Drizzle ORM.

faq.question: Which language is shown in the examples?
faq.answer: The examples use TypeScript with Drizzle ORM imports.

faq.question: Does the skill include complete table schemas?
faq.answer: No. It points to db/schema.ts and lists table names, but it does not include full definitions.

faq.question: Can it run database migrations by itself?
faq.answer: No. It documents migration commands, but users or agents must choose whether to run them.

faq.question: Is DATABASE_URL safe to share?
faq.answer: No. DATABASE_URL can contain credentials and should stay in private environment configuration.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for production changes?
faq.answer: It can support planning, but production changes need review, backups, and environment checks.
